rulings.Case Title: MINHALL, INC.A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. RICHARD A. CHRISTENSEN, AN INDIVIDUAL, DOING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendants Richard A. Christensen And Bds Couture, Inc. , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANTs RICHARD CHRISTENSEN and BDS COUTURE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. The facts conclusively established by the October 23, 2014 discovery order show Defendants are entitled to summary judgment as a matter of law. Plaintiffs admit the first cause of action is moot because BDS has declared bankruptcy. Plaintiff's contention that the established facts do not show the dollar amount of the reduced rent which Plaintiff agreed to accept from Defendant is meritless; Fact B of the October 23, 2014 Order establishes that Plaintiff accepted all payments made by Defendants as payment in full. Similarly, Plaintiff's claim that Plaintiff is entitled to property tax and insurance payments is also meritless; these payments are defined as rent in the lease and therefore covered by the established facts. Finally, Plaintiff's contention that the established facts do not show it released Defendant Christensen from his Guaranty is irrelevant; if no money is owed under the lease there is nothing Plaintiff can collect from Christensen under a Guaranty theory. Defendants to prepare and submit an order approved as to form = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: STAN HELLER VS. CANDU CAPITAL GROUP LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Prayer For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, line 8. DEFENDANT CANDU CAPITAL GROUP LLC, RITU VOHRA and ARJUN DUTT's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs' Prayer For Punitive Damages Off calendar as moot. First Amended Complaint filed March 23, 2015. = (501/REQ)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015, line 9. DEFENDANTs CINDY PON and ANGELA PON's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Defendants fail to meet their burden of showing that the gravamen of Plaintiff's complaint is targeted at a protected activity. Defendants allege the complaint was filed in retaliation for an unlawful detainer action and judgment, but no action or judgment was ever filed. Defendants provide no support for their theory that this lawsuit was filed in retaliation for their service of a 60 day Notice to Quit, and the complaint itself does not so claim. Instead, the complaint pleads that Plaintiff was wrongfully evicted from her apartment in violation of the relevant statutory provisions. "Terminating a tenancy or removing a property from the rental market are not activities taken in furtherance of the constitution rights of petition or free speech." (Moriarty v. Laramar Management Corp. (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 125, 137.) Furthermore, the Court finds that even if a protected activity is implicated, Plaintiffs have carried their burden to show the complaint is "both legally sufficient and supported by a sufficient prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment if the evidence submitted by the plaintiff is credited." (See Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 82, 88-89.) Plaintiffs present evidence showing that Cindy Pon has not registered an ownership interest in the property and that she has not moved into the property in the four months since the purported owner move-in that is the subject of the lawsuit. The Court finds the anti-SLAPP motion was frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. In particular, the Court observes that Defendant's contention that the lawsuit was based on an unlawful detainer action and resulting judgment is completely and utterly without factual support, as no such action was ever filed. The Court therefore awards fees and costs to Plaintiff in the amount of $2,562.50, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5(a). = (501/REQ)
